videos.title: [News Briefing] Today's Top News / SBS
videos.subtitle_summary:
1. 미국과 이란의 종전 협상이 결렬됐고, 트럼프 대통령은 미군이 오늘 밤 11시부터 이란 항구를 출입하는 모든 선박을 봉쇄할 예정이다.
2. 레바논 공격이 계속되고 있으며, 네타냐후 총리는 12일에 레바논 남부를 찾았고, 전쟁은 여전히 계속된다고 강조했습니다.
3. 청주시 한 상가 식당에서 LP 가스통이 폭발하여 15명의 주민이 다쳤으며, 사고 경위를 조사중입니다.

videos.title: 헝가리 총선: 분석가, "헝가리의 승리는 EU 지도자들에게 '안도의 한숨'을 안겨줬다" | APT
videos.subtitle_summary:
1. Viktor Orbán, Hungarian leader, lost the election strongly despite open support from the US and covert support from Russia.
2. His loss is seen as a relief by EU leaders and a sign that illiberal democracies may struggle to remain in power.
3. The outcome of the election sends a strong message to governments like the US and Russia that there's a limit to their influence on elections within the EU member states.

videos.title: “오늘의 유럽은 헝가리다”—von der Leyen, Orbán의 패배, Peter Magyar의 역사적인 승리를 축하 |AC1B
videos.subtitle_summary:
1. Viktor Orban, Hungary's long-term leader, has lost the election after 16 years in power.
2. Peter Magyar, the winner, has promised to improve relations with Brussels and implement reforms to release frozen funds.
3. The victory is seen as a win for fundamental freedoms and a step towards strengthening the European Union, with expectations for progress on various issues benefiting Hungary.

videos.title: "나는 크렘린, 워싱턴 D.C., 브뤼셀에서 만들어지지 않았습니다" — 헝가리인, 선거 개입 의혹 일축 | AC1B
videos.subtitle_summary:
1. Hungarians reinforced Hungary's place in Europe and the EU during a recent vote, rejecting attempts to lead the country out of the EU by the current or resigning corrupt government.
2. Over 50,000 volunteers played a significant role in changing the regime over the past two years, resulting in the creation of the strongest party in Hungary with an unprecedented mandate.
3. World leaders congratulated Hungary for standing up courageously for a free, independent, and sovereign nation amidst challenging conditions.

videos.title: [On-site] Ships Turned Around… What About Korean Vessels? Current Situation in Hormuz Ahead of 'B...
videos.subtitle_summary:
1. The US is expected to impose a blockade on the Strait of Hormuz within the next few hours, and current traffic has come to a halt. A British maritime information company analyzed that the blockade announced by Trump has effectively stopped shipping. An oil tanker with a crew of 173 Koreans is among the ships stuck in the Persian Gulf. The situation is unclear as to when these vessels will be able to escape.
2. Additionally, there's also danger in the Indian Ocean, where a ship was approached by an armed group yesterday near Yemen's Hodeidah in the southern Red Sea. The UK maritime trade body reported that an armed boat with around 10 men tried to stop the ship and board it, but was repelled after warning shots were fired. The incident is believed to be linked to the Houthis, who control the region.
3. Tensions are escalating as the Houthis have threatened to expand military operations if the US and Israel resume attacks against them. This also raises concerns about the safety of the Indian Ocean, which could become an alternative route for bypassing the Strait of Hormuz blockade.
